title: Hopa Ports Takes Steps to Secure Future Of HamiltonГўв‚¬в„ўS Biox Biodiesel Facility
content:
HOPA Ports has taken action to secure the future of the biodiesel facility in Hamilton previously operated by Biox, an asset with a 67 million litre annual capacity that has supported over 160 jobs and provided Ontario with energy autonomy for two decades. Recognizing its strategic value, HOPA Ports finalized an agreement on August 15th to secure the plantГўв‚¬в„ўs assets, preventing their liquidation and maintaining the facility's readiness to resume operations. HOPA is now actively seeking a new operator to restart production.

This effort is bolstered by recent regulatory and government support. The Ontario Ministry of Environment, Conservation and Parks has enacted a new amendment to support the use of Canadian-produced biodiesel, creating a more viable market for local producers. HOPA Ports President and CEO Ian Hamilton praised these collaborative efforts from both provincial and federal governments for sending a clear message of support for CanadaГўв‚¬в„ўs renewable energy sector.

Returning the facility to production is expected to support local jobs, provide a local source of biodiesel for suppliers, and fortify OntarioГўв‚¬в„ўs agricultural sector by maintaining a reliable market for local feedstock. This initiative also aligns with goals for the transition to marine biofuels.
author: Analysts Account
